game_name: Indiana Jones and the Temple of Doom

danger_signals.meaning: immediate combat threat; player must avoid or defeat
instant-death or damage zones; requires precision movement

danger_signals.visual_form: screen transition or arena design change

safety_signals.meaning: intended safe traversal; reward for skill execution
opportunity to strengthen position; risk/reward interaction

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: skull iconography implied; Mola Ram as primary enemy visual
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: enemy sprites representing cult followers, thugs
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: dark temple spaces, ritual chambers, rope bridge arena

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: temple exterior and entrance areas with moderate obstacle density
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: enemies patrol or wait; hazards are stationary or predictable

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: deeper temple interior with increased visual complexity and darker palette
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: increased enemy frequency; more dynamic obstacle patterns

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: arena-specific design (rope bridge for Mola Ram sequence); clear player vs. antagonist spatial relationship
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: boss entity displays pattern-based attacks; enemy AI becomes predictable but lethal

environmental_storytelling.example: temple interior design communicates cult location and oppressive atmosphere through visual style
descent into temple depths mirrors story progression and escalating danger
cult followers and obstacles placed to suggest active temple operations and ritual activity

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: responds to input with platformer-standard jump and directional movement
patrol or stationary; no immediate aggression display
attack pattern initiation; sprite animation suggests aggression
predictable attack pattern; large sprite suggests dominance over player; rope bridge setting limits movement options

notes: This 1988 NES adaptation of the 1985 arcade game features condensed level design and a definitive ending absent from the arcade version. The game communicates world-state primarily through traditional 8-bit platformer visuals: sprite-based enemies, tile-based level design, and environmental hazards. Faction identity is minimal but implied through enemy sprite styling and environmental context (temple setting). The rope bridge Mola Ram encounter represents the primary boss/narrative climax. Limited source data restricts detailed analysis of specific visual communication mechanics; extraction relies on inferred platformer conventions and game structure.
